methods.description: Preparation of sediment samples by air-drying, sieving, and homogenization for subsequent laboratory analyses.

steps.description: A total of 74 samples were taken for further laboratory treatment.
Analyses were performed using the fine-earth fraction (≤2.0 mm) of air-dried samples.
steps.searchable_summary: Air-dry sediment samples and select fine-earth fraction (<= 2.0 mm)

steps.description: The sediment colours were measured using a spectrophotometer (Konica Minolta CM-5) on sieved (<2000 µm), homogenized, and dried samples [61,62].
steps.searchable_summary: Sieve samples (<2000 µm), homogenize, and dry for colour measurement

methods.description: Measurement of sediment colour using a spectrophotometer to determine the L* value, indicating light extinction.

steps.description: The sediment colours were measured using a spectrophotometer (Konica Minolta CM-5) on sieved (<2000 µm), homogenized, and dried samples [61,62].
steps.searchable_summary: Measure sediment colour using a spectrophotometer

steps.description: In this study L* values were used to indicate the extinction of light (0 = absolute black, to 100 = absolute white) [61,63].
steps.searchable_summary: Extract L* values from colour measurements

methods.method_name: Carbon Content Analysis (TC, CaCO3, TIC, TOC)
methods.description: Determination of total carbon (TC), calcium carbonate (CaCO3), inorganic carbon (TIC), and organic carbon (TOC) content in sediment samples using elemental analysis and calcimetry.

steps.description: Total carbon (TC) was measured on milled samples using an Elementar CN-analyser vario EL cube.
steps.searchable_summary: Mill sediment samples for elemental analysis

steps.description: Total carbon (TC) was measured on milled samples using an Elementar CN-analyser vario EL cube.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure Total Carbon (TC) content using a CN analyser

steps.description: CaCO3 content was determined using an Eijkelkamp Scheibler calcimeter.
steps.searchable_summary: Determine Calcium Carbonate (CaCO3) content using a calcimeter

steps.description: Inorganic carbon content (TIC) was calculated by multiplying CaCO3 by 0.12 [64,65].
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate Inorganic Carbon (TIC) from CaCO3 content

steps.description: Organic carbon content (TOC) was calculated by subtracting the TIC from the TC.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate Organic Carbon (TOC) by subtracting TIC from TC

methods.description: Determination of particle size distribution using laser diffraction after organic matter removal and dispersion, including calculation of grain size frequency using Mie theory and Fraunhofer approximation, and calculation of Delta GSD (∆GSD).

steps.description: Organic matter was removed by treating the samples with 0.7 mL 15% hydrogen peroxide (H2O2) at 70 • C for several hours.
steps.searchable_summary: Remove organic matter from samples using hydrogen peroxide treatment
outputs: sediment samples with organic matter removed

steps.description: To keep particles dispersed, the samples were treated with 1.25 mL Na4P2O7 (0.1 mol·L−1 ) for 12 h on an overhead shaker [66,67].
steps.searchable_summary: Disperse particles using sodium pyrophosphate treatment
inputs: sediment samples with organic matter removed

steps.description: Particle size distribution was measured using a Laser Diffraction Particle Size Analyzer (Beckman Coulter LS 13 320).
steps.searchable_summary: Measure particle size distribution using laser diffraction

steps.description: The grain size distribution was determined by applying the Mie theory (Fluid RI: 1.33; Sample RI: 1.55; Imaginary RI: 0.1) [68].
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate grain size frequency distribution using Mie theory

steps.description: The ∆GSD is the difference between grain size frequency calculated with the Fraunhofer approximation and grain size frequency calculated with the Mie theory [69].
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate grain size frequency distribution using Fraunhofer approximation

steps.description: The ∆GSD is the difference between grain size frequency calculated with the Fraunhofer approximation and grain size frequency calculated with the Mie theory [69].
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate Delta GSD (∆GSD) as the difference between Fraunhofer and Mie grain size frequencies

steps.description: For numerical values, the deviations were additionally calculated for the grain size range 0.2 to 0.3 µm.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate Delta GSD (∆GSD) for the 0.2 to 0.3 µm grain size range
